abstract: The seed data that determines the random source’s behavior.
overview:
## Discussion

Any two random sources initialized with the same seed data will generate the same sequence of random numbers. To replicate the behavior of an existing [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKARC4RandomSource] instance, read this property and then create a new instance by passing the resulting data to the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKARC4RandomSource/init(seed:)] initializer.

For a source of high-entropy seed data, see the [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Security/SecRandomCopyBytes(_:_:_:)] function.

# seed

**Instance Property**

The seed data that determines the random source’s behavior.

## Declaration

```swift
var seed: Data { get set }
```

## Discussion

Any two random sources initialized with the same seed data will generate the same sequence of random numbers. To replicate the behavior of an existing [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKARC4RandomSource] instance, read this property and then create a new instance by passing the resulting data to the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKARC4RandomSource/init(seed:)] initializer.

For a source of high-entropy seed data, see the [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Security/SecRandomCopyBytes(_:_:_:)] function.
title: seed
